nav.betabar: This service is in beta — please report issues on <forumLink>the Forum</forumLink>.

home.headline: Let's see if your Matrix server is reachable.
home.lead: Type your homeserver's domain below. We run a few checks from outside your network and walk you through anything that needs attention.

form.domainHint: The bit after the colon in your Matrix handle. If you're @alice:example.com, your domain is example.com.
form.statsLabel: Contribute this result to the public statistics page. We hash your domain before storing — never the raw name.

checks.title: What this checks
checks.step1Detail: Federation handshake on port 8448 or its delegated equivalent.
checks.step2Detail: Client-server API discovery and version probe.

help.title: Need a hand?
help.body: The docs walk through running a homeserver gently — no jargon required up front.

footer.meta: A free, open-source diagnostic tool for Matrix homeserver operators. Self-hostable under AGPL-3.0.

loggedOut.headline: Know the moment your server goes down.
loggedOut.lead: Add any number of Matrix homeservers to watch. We check federation every five minutes and email you when something breaks. You get a reminder every 12 hours while it stays down, and a confirmation when it recovers.

failure.title: Instant failure notification
failure.body: One email when federation breaks, then a reminder every 12 hours while it stays down.

recovery.title: Recovery confirmation
recovery.body: A follow-up when the server comes back so you know it's resolved.

recipients.title: Multiple recipients
recipients.body: Notify any set of verified addresses — ops team, on-call, or just yourself.

loggedOut.ctaNote: Your account is used only to deliver alert emails — no Matrix login required.

authed.headline: Server alerts
authed.lead: Get a quiet email the moment a homeserver becomes unreachable — and another when it recovers. Watch as many servers as you like; notify any combination of addresses for each. Email-only for now; Matrix-room delivery is on the roadmap.

form.title: Add a new alert
form.description: We probe the server every five minutes. The first failure triggers a notification; we won't spam you while it stays down.
form.notifyHint: Pick one or more. Add additional addresses from your account settings.

table.footerNote: One address can be on any number of alerts. Removing an alert never deletes the address itself.

edit.headline: Edit alert for
edit.lead: Pick which changes you want to hear about, who should hear about them, and how often. Saving overwrites the existing alert.
edit.watchDescription: You can mix and match. We send one email per event, never one per check.

uptime.title: Connectivity / downtime
uptime.detail: Notify when federation or the client-server API stops responding. Probed every 5 minutes.
uptime.hint: The original alert. Most people want this.

rename.title: Server name change
rename.detail: Notify if the homeserver implementation reported via /_matrix/federation/v1/version changes — e.g. a Synapse server suddenly identifies as Dendrite.
rename.hint: Useful for spotting account takeovers or accidental rollouts.

version.title: Version update
version.detail: Notify when the version string reported by /_matrix/federation/v1/version changes — typically an upgrade.
version.hint: Quiet by default — most useful for operators tracking peer servers.

tlsChange.title: TLS certificate change
tlsChange.detail: Notify if the TLS certificate fingerprint changes for any federation or client endpoint.
tlsChange.hint: Flags re-issued or rotated certificates. Some renewals are expected; this is a heads-up, not a problem.

tlsExpiry.title: TLS certificate expiry (≤ 14 days)
tlsExpiry.detail: Notify when any certificate in the chain has 14 days or fewer remaining. Repeats daily once tripped.
tlsExpiry.hint: A safety net even if your renewal cron usually handles it.

edit.notifyHint: Tap to toggle. Manage the address list itself from your account settings.

quietHours.description: Hold notifications during this window, applied to each recipient in their own timezone. Critical downtime always sends regardless. Each recipient's timezone comes from their account email settings.

login.headline: Welcome back.
login.lead: An account is only used for downtime alerts.
login.magicDescription: We'll email a one-time link that signs you in without a password. Valid for 1 hour.
login.createAccountMagic: Don't have an account? Create one
login.disclaimer: The Connectivity Tester does not log in with Matrix; this account exists only to deliver alert emails for the homeservers you watch.

sponsor.text: This service is free to use. If it's saved you a headache, consider supporting development.

account.title: Your account
account.leadAfter: . This account is used only to deliver homeserver downtime alerts — it is not a Matrix account and never talks to your homeserver.

emails.title: Email addresses
emails.description: Your primary address is used to sign in. Any verified address can be picked as a recipient when configuring an alert. Adding an address sends a one-click verification email; it stays in a pending state until you click the link.
emails.addDescription: We'll send a verification link valid for 24 hours.

password.title: Password
password.hasPasswordDescription: Change the password used for email-and-password sign-in. We use this alongside magic links — either is enough on its own to get in.
password.noPasswordDescription: You're currently using magic links only. Set up a password to enable email-and-password sign-in.
password.tooWeak: ✗ Too weak — aim for at least Fair
password.setupSuccess: Check your email — a setup link has been sent.

export.title: Download your data
export.description: A single JSON file containing everything we hold about you: account record, email addresses, alert subscriptions and notification history.
export.gdpr: Provided under GDPR Article 20 · Generated on demand · Typically < 50 KB.

delete.title: Delete account

items.profile: your profile and all email addresses;

delete.statsNote: Public, aggregated statistics you opted into are anonymous and remain in place.
